and it also provides for the improvement of working 
conditions within the factory premises. Section 2 of the 
1948 Act is the interpretation clause. Apart from others, 
354 
it provides the definition of “worker” under Section 2(l) 
of the 1948 Act, to mean a person employed, directly or 
through any other agency, whether for wages or not, in 
any manufacturing or cleaning process.”
(Emphasis supplied)
22. In S.M. Datta v. State of Gujarat & Anr., (2001) 7 SCC 659, this 
Court held as under:- 
“19. … The Factories Act, 1948 cannot but be ascribed 
to be a beneficial piece of legislation and the requirement 
of Section 61, in particular, sub-sections (1) and (2) of 
Section 61 can be easily deciphered since the intent stands 
clear enough to indicate that an adult worker must know 
his daily placement and daily workings beforehand — this 
placement beforehand is the requirement of the statute in
